front: What is an Entity in ER Diagram?
back: An entity is a real-world object or concept that has independent existence and can be uniquely identified. Examples: Student, Employee, Product. It is represented as a rectangle in ER diagrams.

front: What is an Attribute in ER Diagram?
back: An attribute is a characteristic or property of an entity that describes it. Examples: Student has attributes like Roll_No, Name, Age, Address. Represented as ovals.

front: What is a Relationship in ER Diagram?
back: A relationship is an association or connection between two or more entities. It describes how entities interact with each other. Represented as diamonds in ER diagrams.
An attribute that can be divided into smaller sub-parts representing more basic attributes with independent meanings. Example: Address can be divided into Street, City, State, Zip.
An attribute that holds a single value for each occurrence of an entity. Example: Age, Mobile_No. It has only one value for each entity instance.
An attribute that can have multiple values for a single entity. Example: Phone_No (a person may have multiple phone numbers). Represented by double ovals.
An attribute whose value can be derived from other related attributes. Example: Age can be derived from Date_of_Birth. Represented by dashed ovals.
An attribute that uniquely identifies each entity in an entity set. Example: Roll_No for Student entity. Represented by underline in ER diagrams.

front: What is Cardinality in ER Diagrams?
back: Cardinality specifies the number of instances of one entity that can be associated with a single instance of another entity. Types: One-to-One, One-to-Many, Many-to-Many.
It specifies whether the existence of an entity depends on its being related to another entity. Types: Total (mandatory) participation and Partial (optional) participation.
An entity that does not have a key attribute of its own and depends on a strong entity for identification. Example: Dependent entity depends on Employee entity. Represented by double rectangle.
An entity that has its own key attribute and can exist independently. It can uniquely identify its instances. Example: Department, Student. Represented by single rectangle.
A relationship where an entity is related to itself. Example: Employee manages Employee (hierarchical structure). Also called unary relationship.
An entity used to convert a Many-to-Many relationship into two One-to-Many relationships. It inherits primary keys from the related entities and becomes a strong entity.

front: Who developed ER Model and in which year?
back: The Entity-Relationship (ER) Model was developed by Peter Chen in 1976. It provides a visual representation of entities, attributes, and relationships for database design.
